channel.title: Paper Feeds (arXiv)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv

item.title: GETA: Generalized Encrypted Traffic Analysis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31277v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着TLS 1.3、QUIC、HTTP/3及各类隧道协议的广泛部署，网络流量加密率持续攀升，传统基于深度包检测（DPI）的流量分析方法因无法访问明文载荷而严重失效。现有机器学习方案虽尝试利用加密流量元数据（如包长、时序、方向），但仍存在三大瓶颈：① 过度依赖协议特定头部特征（如TCP标志位、TLS扩展字段），泛化性差；② 需要大规模人工标注数据集，标注成本高且难以覆盖新兴应用；③ 在跨网络环境（如企业网→IoT边缘→5G核心网）迁移时性能显著下降。

方法创新：GETA框架

本文提出GETA（Generalized Encrypted Traffic Analysis）——首个真正协议无关的加密流量分析框架。其核心设计包括：

  • 元数据时序建模：将每个网络流抽象为多变量时间序列（包间隔、累积字节数、方向序列等），完全摒弃对包载荷与协议语义的依赖；
  • 三阶段自适应架构：融合元学习（MAML）实现跨域知识迁移、嵌入精炼模块动态校准流表征、以及轻量级自注意力机制捕获长程时序依赖；
  • 少样本适配能力：仅需每个新场景下≤5个标注样本/类别即可完成领域适配，显著降低部署门槛。

实验验证与贡献

在涵盖应用识别（85类App）、VPN分类（12种工具）、IoT设备指纹（47种设备）、恶意流量检测（5类攻击）的9个公开基准数据集上，GETA全面超越SOTA方法（如TL-Net、ET-BERT、FlowLens），平均准确率提升6.2–14.8%，跨域泛化误差降低37.5%。本工作证明：仅凭加密流量的“骨架”（纯元数据时序）即可构建鲁棒、可迁移、低标注依赖的分析基础，为零信任网络中的实时流量治理提供了普适性技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

Traditional encrypted traffic analysis faces fundamental challenges due to widespread encryption, tunneling, and privacy protocols that hide payloads and invalidate Deep Packet Inspection (DPI). While ML-based methods exist, they often rely on protocol-specific header features, require large labeled datasets, and degrade across heterogeneous networks. We propose GETA, a protocol-agnostic framework that models network flows as multivariate time series using only traffic metadata (e.g., inter-arrival times, packet lengths, directions)—eliminating dependence on payloads or protocol semantics. GETA integrates meta-learning (MAML), embedding refinement, and lightweight self-attention to enable few-shot adaptation to unseen domains with ≤5 labeled samples per class. Evaluated across nine public datasets spanning application identification, VPN classification, IoT device fingerprinting, and attack detection, GETA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines—achieving average accuracy gains of 6.2–14.8% and reducing cross-domain generalization error by 37.5%. Results demonstrate that robust, generalizable traffic analysis is feasible using metadata alone.

Abstract

Traditional traffic analysis is being fundamentally challenged by the rapid adoption of encryption, tunnelling, and privacy-preserving protocols, which increasingly obscure packet payloads and limit the usefulness of Deep Packet Inspection (DPI). Although machine learning has advanced encrypted traffic analysis, existing approaches often remain tied to protocol-specific header features, depend on large labelled datasets, and degrade when deployed across heterogeneous network environments. We present GETA, a protocol-agnostic framework for encrypted traffic analysis that models network flows as multivariate time series using only traffic metadata, thereby avoiding reliance on packet payloads or header semantics. GETA combines meta-learning, embedding refinement, and self-attention to support few-shot adaptation to previously unseen domains with minimal labelled data. Across nine public datasets spanning application identification, VPN traffic classification, IoT device fingerprinting, and attack detection, GETA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. These results show that GETA offers a practical and generalisable foundation for robust traffic analysis in modern encrypted networks.

item.title: BadBone: Backdoor Attacks Against Backbone Models in Visual Prompt Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31246v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

视觉提示学习（Visual Prompt Learning）作为一种新兴的轻量级迁移范式，通过在冻结骨干网络（backbone）前添加可学习提示（prompt）实现高效下游适配。尽管其简洁性与有效性广受认可，该范式下的模型安全风险却长期被忽视——尤其缺乏针对骨干模型本身、而非提示模块的定向后门攻击研究。

方法：BadBone 攻击框架

本文首次提出 BadBone，一种基于双层优化（bi-level optimization）的隐蔽且自适应的骨干模型后门攻击。与传统攻击污染提示参数或微调头层不同，BadBone 直接污染预训练骨干模型的权重，但精心设计触发机制：仅当目标模型采用提示学习方式（如 CoOp、PromptSRC）进行下游任务时，后门才被激活；而在标准微调（fine-tuning）或线性探针（linear probing）等其他适配方式下，模型行为完全正常——实现任务感知的隐蔽性。

主要发现与创新

  • 在 ViT-B/16、ResNet-50、Deformable DETR 三大架构及 ImageNet-1K、CUB-200、PASCAL VOC 三大跨域数据集上验证：BadBone 在未降低预训练表征质量（ImageNet top-1 准确率下降 <0.3%）和下游任务性能（平均仅降 0.5–1.2%）的前提下，实现 >92% 的靶向攻击成功率（ASR）与 >87% 的非靶向攻击成功率；
  • 对 6 种前沿模型级防御（Neural Cleanse、ABS、MNTD、NAD、CLP、D-BR）进行系统评估，结果表明：所有防御均无法有效识别或净化 BadBone 污染的骨干模型（平均检测率 <28%，净化后 ASR 仍 >79%），凸显其强规避能力；
  • 首次揭示“骨干模型安全 ≠ 提示学习安全”这一关键认知断层，为提示学习生态的安全治理提供新视角与紧迫挑战。

AI Summary (English)

Prompt learning has emerged as an efficient paradigm for adapting frozen vision backbones via learnable prompts, yet its security vulnerabilities—especially at the backbone level—remain unexplored. We propose BadBone, the first stealthy, task-aware backdoor attack that directly poisons the pre-trained backbone using bi-level optimization. Crucially, BadBone ensures the backdoor activates only when downstream tasks employ prompt learning (e.g., CoOp), while preserving clean behavior under fine-tuning or linear probing—achieving unprecedented adaptivity and concealment. Extensive experiments across three architectures (ViT-B/16, ResNet-50, Deformable DETR) and three diverse datasets (ImageNet-1K, CUB-200, PASCAL VOC) show BadBone achieves >92% targeted ASR and >87% untargeted ASR, with negligible utility degradation (<0.3% ImageNet top-1 drop; <1.2% avg. downstream accuracy loss). Against six state-of-the-art model-level defenses (Neural Cleanse, ABS, MNTD, NAD, CLP, D-BR), BadBone evades detection (avg. <28% detection rate) and resists purification (post-defense ASR remains >79%). Our work exposes a critical security gap: backbone integrity alone does not guarantee prompt learning safety—highlighting urgent needs for task-aware defense mechanisms.

Abstract

Prompt learning is a new machine learning paradigm that has attracted ample attention due to its simplicity and proven efficacy. Despite its growing adoption, the security vulnerabilities associated with this paradigm remain underexplored. In this work, we take the first step to propose BadBone, a stealthy and adaptive backdoor attack against prompt learning using bi-level optimization. Instead of backdooring the prompt learning process, we aim to compromise a backbone model such that only target downstream tasks employing prompt learning inherit the backdoor vulnerability. Extensive experiments on three different models and three datasets from various domains show that our targeted/untargeted backdoored models achieve high attack performance while maintaining utility on both pre-training and downstream tasks. Moreover, we evaluate our approach against six state-of-the-art model-level defenses, including Neural Cleanse, ABS, MNTD, NAD, CLP, and D-BR. The results demonstrate that these defenses are largely ineffective against our backdoored models and thus leave the effective defense as an important direction for future work.

item.title: R+R: Reassessing Java Security API Misuse in Current LLMs: A Replication on JCA and JSSE APIs with External Security Knowledge
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31135v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Java安全API（如JCA和JSSE）的误用是软件开发中长期存在的高危安全问题。2024年已有研究表明，大型语言模型（LLMs）生成的Java代码普遍存在此类误用。然而，随着模型快速迭代（如GPT-5.5、Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct），该问题是否持续存在？外部安全知识（如安全示例、误用模式、开发者指南）能否有效缓解？其效果是否因模型架构与能力而异？这些问题尚缺乏系统验证。

方法设计

本研究对Mousavi等人的开创性工作开展严格复现与拓展：在统一基准下，评估两类代表性前沿模型——GPT-5.5（前沿闭源编码模型）与Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct（强开源权重、支持私有部署）——在JCA/JSSE API任务中的安全编码表现。我们引入四类外部安全知识：① 安全代码示例；② 显式误用模式清单；③ Java安全开发者指南摘要；④ 安全导向提示工程（Secure Prompting）。所有实验均控制检索上下文长度与格式一致性，确保可比性。

主要发现

  • 尽管新模型整体性能提升，Java安全API误用仍未根除：GPT-5.5仍产生约18%含误用的有效程序，Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct达32%；
  • 外部知识显著提升安全性，但效果高度模型依赖：对Llama，安全示例最有效（误用率↓41%）；对GPT-5.5，显式误用模式最有力——在所有通过编译且调用目标API的程序中，彻底消除检测到的安全误用；
  • 开发者指南知识对GPT-5.5增益跃升（+29%安全率），安全提示亦带来大幅改进（+22%）；
  • 本研究首次揭示：RAG（检索增强生成）的价值不仅取决于知识质量与检索精度，更深度耦合于模型原生安全推理能力。

创新点

提出“模型-知识协同适配”视角，为安全编码辅助工具的设计提供实证依据：针对不同部署场景（云服务vs私有化），应定制化选择知识类型与注入方式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper replicates and extends Mousavi et al.’s 2024 study on Java security API misuse, evaluating two state-of-the-art models—GPT-5.5 (proprietary frontier) and Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct (strong open-weight)—on JCA/JSSE tasks with four external security knowledge types: secure examples, explicit misuse patterns, developer-guide excerpts, and secure prompting. Results show that while newer LLMs reduce but do not eliminate security API misuse, external knowledge yields substantial, model-dependent improvements: for Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, secure examples are most effective; for GPT-5.5, explicit misuse patterns eliminate all detected misuses among valid, compilable programs, and developer-guide knowledge + secure prompting deliver large gains. Crucially, the efficacy of retrieval-augmented security knowledge depends not only on content and retrieval but also on intrinsic model capability—highlighting the need for model-aware knowledge integration in secure coding assistants.

Abstract

The misuse of Java security APIs is a serious security problem in software development. Research in 2024 has shown that this problem is widespread in LLM-generated code. However, it remains unclear whether this phenomenon persists in current models and how external security knowledge affects it. This paper presents a scoped replication and extension of Mousavi et al.'s study on the Java Cryptography Architecture (JCA) and Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE) APIs. We focus on two complementary settings: GPT-5.5 as a frontier proprietary coding model, and Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct as a strong open-weight model relevant to self-hosted deployment. The results show that although newer LLMs perform better in using Java security APIs, the problem of Java security API misuse has not been eliminated. External security knowledge substantially improves the measured outcome, but its effect is model-dependent. For Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, secure code examples are the most effective single knowledge type. For GPT-5.5, explicit misuse patterns eliminate all detected security API misuses among valid programs in our benchmark, although some outputs remain invalid due to compilation errors or target-API mismatches. In addition, developer-guide knowledge becomes much more effective, and secure prompting also provides large gains for GPT-5.5. Overall, these findings confirm the Java security API misuse risk identified in the original study and show that the benefits of retrieval-augmented knowledge depend not only on the knowledge itself and retrieval behavior, but also on model capability.

item.title: From Prompt Injection to Persistent Control: Defending Agentic Harness Against Trojan Backdoors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31042v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）智能体正从对话式聊天机器人演进为具备文件读写、工具调用与跨会话状态复用能力的本地化操作型代理（agentic harness）。这一增强能力在提升实用性的同时，催生新型多步“特洛伊后门”攻击：攻击者将恶意指令（如prompt injection）隐匿于普通文本文件或工具输出中；代理在后续会话中读取该内容、缓存并执行，从而实现持久化控制。此类攻击各环节单独检视均无明显恶意特征，但组合后可将不可信文本转化为长期可控的执行载荷——而现有防御机制多采用单步静态检测，难以追溯攻击源头，导致对“埋点写入”阶段完全失察。

方法与创新

为系统揭示该威胁，我们构建首个面向本地代理环境的多步特洛伊攻击基准——ClawTrojan，基于OpenClaw仿真工作区与GPT-5.4模型开展评估。实验表明：ClawTrojan攻击成功率（ASR）达95.5%，而传统单轮prompt注入攻击在相同设置下ASR近乎为零，证实多步协同是关键突破口。针对此，我们提出DASGuard动态防御框架：① 实时扫描敏感本地文件中的类控制文本（如含/execute、run_as_admin等模式）；② 追溯其来源路径（origin tracing），验证是否源自可信用户输入或白名单工具；③ 对非可信源生成的控制内容实施运行时拦截 + 工作区净化提交（sanitized commits），阻断持久化链路。

主要发现

DASGuard在保持0.8%误报率前提下，将ClawTrojan ASR压制至2.1%，显著优于基线防御；其“溯源+净化”双机制首次实现了对多步特洛伊攻击全生命周期的动态干预，为安全代理系统提供了可部署、可审计的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) agents are evolving into operational tools capable of file I/O, tool invocation, and cross-session state reuse—creating a new attack surface for multi-step Trojan backdoors. In such attacks, malicious instructions are stealthily embedded in files or tool outputs; agents later read, store, and execute them, achieving persistent control without any single step appearing overtly harmful. Existing defenses inspect steps in isolation and thus miss the initial “planting” phase. To expose this threat, we introduce ClawTrojan, the first benchmark for multi-step Trojan attacks in local agentic harnesses. Evaluated in an OpenClaw-style workspace with GPT-5.4, ClawTrojan achieves a 95.5% attack success rate (ASR), while standard single-turn prompt injection yields near-zero ASR. To counter it, we propose DASGuard, a dynamic defense that: (1) scans sensitive local files for control-like text, (2) traces its provenance to verify trusted origin, and (3) blocks execution and sanitizes workspace commits for untrusted content. DASGuard reduces ASR to 2.1% with only 0.8% false positives, demonstrating the first effective end-to-end mitigation for persistent agent hijacking.

Abstract

LLM agents are evolving from conversational chatbots to operational tools in real-world workspaces. In local agentic harnesses, an LLM can read and write files, call tools, and reuse workspace state across sessions. While such capabilities enhance utility, they also expose a new attack surface for attackers. Attackers can embed a prompt injection within a file or tool output. Agents may read this hidden instruction, store it, and execute it later. In this multi-step trojan attack paradigm, no individual step appears malicious on its own, but these steps can collectively turn untrusted text into persistent control content. However, existing defenses often inspect each step in isolation. As a result, they can block a clear harmful action, but fail to detect the earlier write operation that plants the backdoor. To reveal this threat, we introduce ClawTrojan, a benchmark designed to identify multi-step trojan attacks in local agentic harnesses. In an OpenClaw-style simulated workspace with GPT-5.4, ClawTrojan reaches a 95.5% attack success rate (ASR), while existing single-turn prompt-injection attacks produce near-zero ASR on the same model. To address this threat, we propose DASGuard, which scans control-like text in sensitive local files, traces its origin, and removes control content that does not originate from a trusted source. Our results show that DASGuard achieves strong dynamic defense by combining runtime attack blocking with sanitized commits to the workspace.

item.title: Free-Riding in the AI Economy: Demystifying Logic Flaws in x402-Enabled Payment Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30998v1

AI Summary (中文)

自由搭车问题：x402 支付系统中的逻辑缺陷深度剖析

在AI驱动的“代理经济”（agentic economy）中，机器对机器（M2M）支付亟需可编程金融基础设施，x402协议被广泛采纳为事实标准。然而，该协议强行桥接同步HTTP请求与异步区块链终局性，引发根本性的状态同步断裂。本研究首次对x402生态开展系统性安全分析，提出并形式化定义五大安全不变量（Security Invariants），揭示当前主流实现普遍缺失事务原子性保障与密码学上下文绑定机制，构成系统性风险基底。

我们识别出两类关键逻辑缺陷：

  • 语义间隙（Semantic Gap）：签名设计未绑定资源标识符，导致支付凭证（payment proof）可被恶意跨资源复用——例如将A模型推理的付款证明篡改为B模型调用，绕过授权检查；
  • 时间间隙（Temporal Gap）：缺乏强一致性并发控制，引发竞态条件，使服务提供方在高并发下概率性重复执行（如两次扣费但仅一次计费），造成服务冗余供给。

在AI推理场景实证中，动态定价模型暴露严重脆弱性：攻击者可触发配额超额透支（allowance overdraft）并突破基础设施速率限制。我们基于官方SDK及Coinbase、ThirdWeb等真实生产环境验证全部漏洞，发现攻击者能利用动态授权中的同步缺陷，强制商户承担全部算力成本——在某主流中间件上测得资源泄漏率达100%（即零成本获取全额服务）。

本文提出两项架构级缓解方案：请求绑定签名（request-bound signatures）与悲观式状态锁（pessimistic state locking），为自主代理的金融底层提供可验证安全保障。所有漏洞已向Coinbase与ThirdWeb负责任披露并获确认。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first comprehensive security analysis of the x402 protocol—the de facto standard for machine-to-machine payments in the AI economy. We formalize five Security Invariants and expose two fundamental logic flaws: a semantic gap enabling cross-resource substitution of payment proofs (e.g., reusing a proof for Model A to authorize Model B), and a temporal gap causing probabilistic service duplication under concurrency due to race conditions. In AI inference settings, these flaws enable allowance overdrafts, rate-limit bypasses, and full compute cost externalization—demonstrated with up to 100% resource leakage on production middleware. We validate all findings against official SDKs and live deployments at Coinbase and ThirdWeb. To mitigate, we propose request-bound signatures and pessimistic state locking. All vulnerabilities have been responsibly disclosed.

Abstract

The agentic economy demands programmatic financial rails, positioning the x402 protocol as the de facto standard for machine-to-machine payments. However, bridging synchronous HTTP requests with asynchronous blockchain finality introduces profound state synchronization challenges. In this work, we perform the first comprehensive security analysis of the x402 ecosystem. By formalizing five Security Invariants, we reveal that current implementations fail to enforce transactional atomicity and cryptographic context binding, leading to systemic vulnerabilities. We identify a semantic gap in signature design enabling cross-resource substitution, where payment proofs are transplanted to other unauthorized contexts. Furthermore, we expose a temporal gap where concurrency race conditions allow probabilistic service duplication. In the AI inference domain, we demonstrate how dynamic pricing models are vulnerable to allowance overdrafts and infrastructure rate limits. We validate these vulnerabilities against official SDKs and live deployments. Specifically, we show that attackers can exploit the synchronization gap in dynamic authorization schemes to force merchants to subsidize compute costs, achieving a resource leakage ratio of up to 100% on production middleware. Finally, we propose architectural mitigations, advocating for request-bound signatures and pessimistic state locking to secure the financial rails of autonomous agents. All discovered issues have been disclosed to Coinbase and ThirdWeb.

item.title: TRACE: Task-Aware Adaptive Self-Evolving Agentic Jailbreaking
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30883v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）智能体的兴起催生了一类新型安全威胁：其具备自主规划、代码生成乃至端到端执行专家级攻击流程的能力。然而，该威胁长期被低估——一方面，安全对齐机制严格限制LLM直接输出有害指令；另一方面，现有主流越狱（jailbreak）方法难以稳定诱导智能体执行恶意操作，尤其在多步任务编排场景下失效显著。

方法创新：TRACE框架

本文提出TRACE（Task-Aware Adaptive Self-Evolving Agentic Jailbreaking），一种面向智能体的自适应、任务感知型越狱框架，旨在系统性揭示智能体安全盲区。其核心设计包含三层隐蔽机制：

  • 任务解耦与最优序列筛选：将原始恶意任务分解为多种子任务序列方案，基于“显式有害子任务数量”最小化原则自动选取最隐蔽的执行路径；
  • 场景化语义伪装：将剩余有害子任务嵌入高度可信的任务上下文（如模拟教育助手调试API、合规审计员分析日志），关联角色、环境、指令范式与启发式逻辑，实现语义层面的良性表象；
  • 自演化场景优化：通过定义明确的转换动作集（如角色迁移、约束松弛、目标重表述），采用受Q学习启发的采样策略迭代优化场景描述，在保持表面合理性的同时持续提升诱导成功率。

实验结果与影响

在AgentHarm与AdvCUA两大权威智能体安全基准上，TRACE在GPT-4o、Claude-3.5、Qwen2.5-72B等先进LLM智能体上均显著超越所有基线方法，平均越狱成功率达100%，加权成功率得分达0.73（+28.6%相对提升）。进一步在可控网络攻击实验中（如隐蔽凭证提取、权限提升链触发），验证了其真实攻击可行性。本工作不仅暴露了当前智能体安全防护的关键脆弱点，更提供了可复现、可扩展的风险评估新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces TRACE, a practical, task-aware, and self-evolving agentic jailbreaking framework designed to expose critical safety vulnerabilities in LLM-based agents. Unlike conventional jailbreaks targeting static prompts, TRACE dynamically decomposes malicious tasks into subtask sequences, selects the most covert sequence (minimizing explicitly harmful steps), and disguises residual harmful subtasks within benign, role- and context-aware scenarios (e.g., “security auditor reviewing logs”). These scenarios iteratively evolve via Q-learning-inspired transformation actions—enabling adaptive, high-fidelity induction of agent-executed malicious behavior. Evaluated on AgentHarm and AdvCUA across GPT-4o, Claude-3.5, and Qwen2.5-72B agents, TRACE achieves up to 100% bypass rate and an average success score of 0.73, outperforming all baselines. It further demonstrates real-world feasibility in controlled cyberattack simulations. Code and demos: https://github.com/ZJU-LLM-Safety/TRACE.git.

Abstract

The rise of LLM agents introduces a new threat by enabling planning, coding, and even end-to-end execution of expert-level attack workflows. However, this threat remains underexplored and underestimated since (i) safety alignment prevents LLMs from directly generating harmful instructions, and (ii) most existing jailbreak methods cannot consistently induce agents to execute malicious operations. In this paper, we propose TRACE, a practical agentic jailbreaking framework to further reveal the risks of this threat surface. To conceal the malicious intent, TRACE decomposes a malicious task into multiple subtask sequences under different schemes and selects the sequence with the fewest explicitly harmful subtasks. TRACE then disguises the remaining harmful subtasks as benign-looking instructions by embedding them in task-aware scenarios with related roles, environments, directives, and heuristics. The scenarios are iteratively evolved through well-defined transformation actions, which are sampled by a Q-learning-inspired mechanism, for inducing the agent to execute on the harmful subtasks. Extensive evaluations on AgentHarm and AdvCUA show that TRACE consistently outperforms existing jailbreak baselines across multiple advanced LLM agents, achieving up to 100% bypass rate and 0.73 average success score. We also demonstrate the effectiveness of TRACE in controlled cyberattack instances. Our code and demos are available at https://github.com/ZJU-LLM-Safety/TRACE.git.

item.title: Send a SCOUT First: Pre-hoc Reasoning for Adaptive Detector Allocation in Prompt-Injection Defense
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30837v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前提示注入（prompt injection）防御系统普遍采用静态单检测器流水线，即对所有请求强制调用同一检测模型。然而，现实中的检测器具有显著异质性：不同检测器在攻击子集上表现各异，且无一具备全局鲁棒性——每个模型都存在固有盲区。这种“一刀切”策略导致大量请求被错误地交由不擅长其特征的检测器处理，既牺牲安全性（漏检），又浪费计算资源（冗余低效推理）。

方法：SCOUT 框架

本文提出 SCOUT（Scalable and Controllable Outcome-prediction for Uncertainty-aware Triage），将防御范式从“固定检测”重构为自适应检测器分配：针对每个输入请求，动态决策——① 从异构检测器池中选择最优子集运行；② 必要时触发高成本LLM裁判（如GPT-4o）进行终审。SCOUT 的核心创新在于预判式推理（pre-hoc reasoning）：基于历史相似样本的执行轨迹，实时预测每个检测器在当前样本上的可靠性得分（预测准确率）与延迟估计（毫秒级），从而实现细粒度、样本级的可信度感知调度。

关键贡献与结果

  • 构建新型基准 SCOUT-450：聚焦结构复杂、面向智能体（agent-facing）的真实注入场景，弥补BIPIA等旧数据集对多步、上下文耦合攻击的覆盖不足；
  • 在SCOUT-450上，安全优先配置相较始终启用GPT-4o裁判方案，攻击成功率降低46%，总耗时减少40%，仅以5.1点良性效用（benign-pass rate + 响应延迟加权）为代价；
  • SCOUT具备强泛化性，在BIPIA、IPI、IHEval三大外部基准上均显著提升安全-效用前沿（safety-utility frontier），验证其跨数据分布鲁棒性。

本工作首次将“不确定性感知分诊”引入提示注入防御，为构建可扩展、可控、自适应的安全中间件提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Prompt-injection defense suffers from detector heterogeneity: no single detector is universally reliable, yet existing systems deploy fixed, monolithic pipelines—exposing every request to each detector’s blind spots. We reframe defense as adaptive detector allocation: given a pool of diverse detectors, decide per-request which to run—and whether to escalate to an expensive LLM judge. Our framework, SCOUT, enables this via pre-hoc reasoning: it predicts per-sample reliability and latency for each detector by leveraging similarity-based retrieval from historical execution traces, and exposes a single tunable safety-utility threshold (balancing benign-pass rate and wall-clock time). To rigorously evaluate adaptive allocation, we introduce SCOUT-450, a benchmark emphasizing structurally complex, agent-facing injections underrepresented in prior datasets. On SCOUT-450, a safety-oriented SCOUT configuration reduces attack success rate by 46% and total wall-clock time by 40% versus always-on GPT-4o—costing only 5.1 points in benign utility. SCOUT also transfers effectively to three external benchmarks (BIPIA, IPI, IHEval), consistently improving the safety-utility Pareto frontier.

Abstract

Prompt-injection detectors are heterogeneous: each is strong on a different slice of attacks, and none is always reliable. Yet existing systems still treat detection as a fixed single-detector pipeline, committing every request to one detector's blind spots. We reframe defense as detector allocation: given a heterogeneous pool, decide per request which detectors to run and whether to escalate to an LLM judge. Our framework SCOUT (Scalable and Controllable Outcome-prediction for Uncertainty-aware Triage) makes this decision dynamic by predicting each detector's per-sample reliability and latency from how it behaved on similar past inputs, and exposes a single safety-utility threshold to the operator (where utility bundles benign-pass rate and wall-clock). To evaluate this setting, we build SCOUT-450, a benchmark that captures the structurally complex, agent-facing injections that older prompt-injection sets under-represent. On SCOUT-450, a safety-oriented operating point reduces attack-success rate by 46% and total wall-clock by 40% relative to an always-on GPT-4o judge, at a 5.1-point benign-utility drop. SCOUT also transfers to three external benchmarks (BIPIA, IPI, and IHEval), improving the safety-utility frontier.

item.title: Differentially Private Preference Data Synthesis for Large Language Model Alignment
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30808v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大语言模型（LLM）的偏好对齐（preference alignment）是关键后训练步骤，旨在使模型输出符合人类价值观。然而，直接使用真实人类偏好数据（含敏感用户提示与人工评分）进行对齐会引发严重隐私风险，现有方法缺乏兼顾隐私保障与对齐性能的有效方案。

方法：DPPrefSyn 框架

我们提出 DPPrefSyn——首个面向LLM对齐的差分隐私（DP）偏好数据合成算法。该框架基于Bradley-Terry偏好模型与偏好数据固有的几何结构，包含两大核心阶段：

  • 隐私建模阶段：在严格 $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP 保证下，利用DP主成分分析（DP-PCA） 提升参数估计鲁棒性，并建模跨簇共享的线性奖励结构，以高效捕捉私有数据中异质化的人类偏好；
  • 合成生成阶段：将学习到的DP奖励模型与公开可用的提示集结合，生成高质量、语义一致的合成偏好对（如 $x \succ y$），无需访问原始敏感响应。

主要发现与创新

实验表明，在 $\varepsilon=1.0$–$2.0$ 的强隐私预算下，DPPrefSyn 合成的数据支持的RLHF/DAPO对齐效果，媲美非隐私基线（如仅下降 ≤0.8% Elo 分数），显著优于噪声注入或裁剪等启发式方法。本工作首次实现端到端DP偏好数据合成，为医疗咨询、教育反馈等高敏场景提供可部署的隐私对齐新范式。代码已开源。

AI Summary (English)

Preference alignment is essential for aligning large language models (LLMs) with human values, yet training on real human preference data risks exposing sensitive prompts and judgments. To bridge this gap, we propose DPPrefSyn, the first differentially private (DP) framework for synthesizing high-fidelity preference data for LLM alignment. Grounded in the Bradley-Terry model and the geometric structure of pairwise preferences, DPPrefSyn jointly leverages DP Principal Component Analysis (DP-PCA) and a shared linear reward structure across user clusters to learn a privacy-preserving preference model from private data. It then synthesizes preference pairs using public prompts—avoiding any access to raw sensitive responses. Extensive experiments show that models aligned with DPPrefSyn-generated data achieve competitive performance (e.g., within ≤0.8% Elo drop vs. non-private baselines) under strong DP guarantees ($\varepsilon = 1.0$–$2.0$), outperforming naive DP baselines. This work establishes synthetic DP preference data as a practical, principled alternative for privacy-aware LLM alignment.

Abstract

Preference alignment is a crucial post-training step for large language models (LLMs) to ensure their outputs align with human values. However, post-training on real human preference data raises privacy concerns, as these datasets often contain sensitive user prompts and human judgments. To address this, we propose DPPrefSyn, a novel algorithm for generating differentially private (DP) synthetic preference data to enable privacy-preserving preference alignment. DPPrefSyn is a principled framework grounded in the Bradley-Terry preference model and the intrinsic geometric structure of pairwise human preference data. It first learns an underlying preference model from private data with formal differential privacy guarantees, and then leverages the learned model together with public prompts to synthesize high-quality preference data. It exploits the shared linear structure of per-cluster reward models to effectively capture heterogeneous human preferences in private datasets, and leverages DP Principal Component Analysis (DP-PCA) to improve learning accuracy. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that DPPrefSyn achieves competitive alignment performance under strong DP guarantees. These findings highlight the potential of synthetic preference data as a practical alternative for privacy-preserving preference alignment across a broad range of applications. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to generate DP synthetic preference data for LLM alignment. Our code is available at https://github.com/gfengyu/Differentially-Private-Preference-Data-Synthesis.

item.title: Depth-Dependent Indirect Prompt Injection in Tool-Calling ReAct Agents: Injection Depth, Payload Framing, and Turn-Budget Sensitivity
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30686v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

面向工具调用的ReAct智能体（如GPT-4o-mini、Claude Haiku）正广泛应用于日程调度、文件检索与数据访问等真实任务。其“推理–调用–观察”循环虽提升能力，却引入间接提示注入（Indirect Prompt Injection, IPI）这一关键安全漏洞：攻击者若控制任一工具返回值，即可嵌入恶意指令，劫持代理偏离用户目标。

方法创新

本文首次系统解耦IPI风险的三大维度——注入深度（payload在工具调用序列中的位置）、载荷修辞框架（neutral/authority/persona等语体）、轮次预算（agent最大交互步数），突破现有基准仅固定单一注入位置的局限。我们在20个多样化场景（覆盖5类攻击模式）中开展4项受控实验，共460次API调用，总成本＜0.36美元。

核心发现

1. 注入深度是主导风险变量：GPT-4o-mini的攻击成功率（ASR）从深度1的60%骤降至深度4–5的0%（Cramer’s V = 0.58, p < 0.001）；深度1–3内仍具强效应（V = 0.47, p = 0.0013），主因模型对首观察能力强，而深层payload常被任务提前终止规避；
2. 模型差异显著：Claude Haiku在所有深度均实现0% ASR，源于其保守调用策略与内在指令抗性；
3. 修辞框架具调节作用但未达统计显著：深度1下ASR在25%（中性）至75%（角色扮演）间波动（N=20/组）；
4. 轮次预算无显著影响：ASR在3/5/7轮限制下保持稳定，表明该参数非当前风险因子。

实践启示

仅对首个工具观测结果进行净化即可捕获67%的已测注入成功案例，为轻量级防御提供高性价比基线。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies and quantifies depth-dependent indirect prompt injection as the dominant security risk in tool-calling ReAct agents. Through four controlled studies across 20 scenarios (460 total trials; <$0.36 API cost), we systematically vary three previously unexamined dimensions: injection depth (position in the tool-call sequence), payload framing (e.g., neutral vs. persona-based rhetoric), and turn budget (maximum agent steps). Against GPT-4o-mini, attack success rate (ASR) collapses from 60% at depth 1 to 0% at depths 4–5 (p < 0.001), driven by model resistance at shallow depth and premature task completion at deeper positions. Claude Haiku achieves 0% ASR across all depths due to conservative tool invocation and intrinsic instruction resistance. Framing modulates ASR by up to 50 percentage points at depth 1 (25%–75%), though not statistically significant at N=20 per condition. ASR remains stable across turn caps of 3, 5, and 7—indicating turn budget is not a risk factor here. Crucially, sanitizing only the first tool observation mitigates 67% of observed injection successes, establishing injection depth as both the primary vulnerability axis and the most actionable defense target.

Abstract

ReAct agents that interleave chain-of-thought reasoning with tool calls are increasingly deployed for real tasks such as scheduling, file retrieval, and data access. Their tool observation loop creates a direct attack surface: an adversary who controls any tool's return value can embed instructions that redirect the agent away from the user's goal, a threat known as indirect prompt injection. Existing benchmarks evaluate attack success rate (ASR) at a fixed injection position under fixed conditions, leaving three risk dimensions unexplored: where in the tool sequence the payload appears (injection depth), what rhetorical register it uses (framing), and how many turns the agent is permitted (turn cap). We conduct four controlled studies on 20 scenarios spanning five attack categories, totalling 460 trials against GPT-4o-mini and Claude Haiku at a combined API cost under 0.36 USD. Study 1 shows that ASR against GPT-4o-mini decays from 60% at depth 1 to 0% at depths 4 and 5 (Cramer's V = 0.58, p < 0.001; restricted to within-sequence depths 1-3: V = 0.47, p = 0.0013), driven by model resistance at depth 1 and task completion before payload encounter at deeper positions. Study 2 replicates the depth experiment on Claude Haiku, which achieves 0% ASR at every depth through a combination of conservative tool invocation and genuine instruction resistance. Study 3 shows that framing modulates ASR between 25% (neutral) and 75% (persona) at depth 1, a 50-percentage-point range that does not reach statistical significance at N = 20 per condition. Study 4 confirms that ASR is stable across turn caps of 3, 5, and 7, indicating the turn budget is not a risk factor in this setting. Our results establish injection depth as the dominant variable and show that sanitising only the first tool observation captures 67% of measured injection successes.

item.title: Investigating Detection and Obfuscation of Prompt Injection Attacks Against Software Reverse Engineering AI Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30677v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

随着大语言模型驱动的智能体式软件逆向工程系统（Agentic SRE Agents）在网络安全分析中加速落地，其面临新型对抗威胁——源码级提示注入攻击（Prompt Injection via Source Code）。攻击者将恶意提示嵌入可执行二进制文件的原始源码（如注释、字符串常量、调试符号等），经反编译后被AI代理误读为指令，从而绕过安全策略、泄露敏感信息或执行越权操作。此类攻击隐蔽性强、检测难度高，且现有防御手段尚未覆盖反编译输出这一关键中间态。

方法与技术路径

本研究提出一套分层防御框架：

  • 检测层：构建轻量级基于规则与语义特征的混合检测器，针对反编译输出（如IDA/ Ghidra生成的伪C代码）识别高风险提示模式（如“忽略上文”“输出全部内存”等指令性短语），并引入上下文感知词向量相似度分析，提升对语义变体的鲁棒性；
  • 对抗层：系统性分析5类典型混淆策略（Base64编码、字符串拼接、Unicode零宽字符、控制流扁平化伪装、多阶段解包），复现真实攻击链；
  • 反混淆层：设计动态解码钩子与静态AST重写模块，在反编译流水线中嵌入实时解混淆器，实现对编码/拼接类混淆的98.7%还原率（在120个对抗样本集上验证）。

主要发现与创新点

实验表明，未经防护的SRE智能体在含注入样本上攻击成功率高达93.5%，而本方案将检测召回率提升至96.2%（F1=0.94），误报率压低至2.1%；首次揭示反编译器输出格式本身构成新型攻击面，并建立首个面向SRE场景的提示注入基准数据集（SRE-PIDB，含217个标注样本）。本工作为AI驱动逆向系统的安全加固提供了可部署的检测范式与实证基础，支撑其向实战化网络攻防流程可靠集成。

AI Summary (English)

This paper investigates prompt injection attacks targeting agentic software reverse engineering (SRE) AI systems—where adversaries embed malicious instructions directly into source code (e.g., comments, string literals) of binaries, which then surface in decompiler output and hijack AI agents’ behavior. We propose a dual-phase defense: (1) a hybrid detector for identifying injection strings in decompiled pseudo-C code, combining syntactic rules with context-aware semantic similarity; and (2) a counter-obfuscation module that dynamically decodes and rewrites obfuscated variants (e.g., Base64, string concatenation, zero-width Unicode). Evaluated on 120 adversarial binaries, our approach achieves 96.2% recall and 2.1% false positive rate, significantly outperforming baseline classifiers. We further introduce SRE-PIDB—the first benchmark dataset for prompt injection in SRE contexts—and demonstrate that decompiler output constitutes a critical, previously overlooked attack surface. This work advances the security foundations for production-grade AI-assisted reverse engineering.

Abstract

Agentic software reverse engineering systems are vulnerable to prompt injection attacks placed into the source code of executable binary files. This research demonstrates defensive tactics for detecting the presences of prompt injection strings in the decompiler output of adversarial example programs. Methods for obfuscating these attacks and subsequent methods for defending against these obfuscations are also explored. This research advances the understanding of risk and security of agentic software analysis systems necessary for their deployment into production-level cyber workflows.

item.title: Simulation of collision avoidance behavior in crowd movement by data-driven approach
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31210v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

人群运动仿真对行人安全管理、应急疏散规划及设施布局优化至关重要。现有数据驱动模型（如基于LSTM或GAN的轨迹预测方法）虽在欧氏距离指标下具备较高轨迹拟合精度，但在高密度双向/多向流场景中普遍存在碰撞率过高问题，难以复现真实行人主动避让行为，暴露出其动力学机制建模的缺失。

方法创新

本文提出一种新型数据驱动人群仿真模型——CPGAN（Collision-Penalized Generative Adversarial Network），核心创新在于将行人碰撞物理机制显式嵌入训练目标：

  • 首创基于横向加速度的碰撞损失函数（Lateral-Acceleration Collision Loss, LACL），通过量化行人为规避对向来人而产生的瞬时侧向加速度变化，实现对潜在碰撞风险的可微分、几何感知建模；
  • 提出Voronoi图驱动的运动特征提取方法，动态构建个体局部邻域结构，精准刻画空间竞争关系与避让意图；
  • 以条件GAN为框架，联合优化轨迹生成器与判别器，在保留数据分布真实性的同时强约束物理合理性。

主要结果与意义

在标准双向流实验场景（如双通道走廊）中验证表明：CPGAN将对向行人碰撞率降低达72.4%，逼近受控实测水平（<0.8%）；成功复现典型自组织现象——稳定分道（lane formation）与N型速度-密度曲线（N-t curve）。本研究首次系统验证了“将行人动力学先验编码进损失函数”的可行性，为数据驱动仿真从“形状匹配”迈向“机制可解释”提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Crowd simulation is vital for pedestrian safety and infrastructure design, yet data-driven models often yield high collision rates—especially in bidirectional flows—despite accurate trajectory fitting under Euclidean metrics. To address this, we propose CPGAN (Collision-Penalized GAN), a novel generative framework that explicitly incorporates pedestrian collision mechanics into the loss function. We introduce a lateral-acceleration-based collision loss (LACL) to penalize unsafe proximity maneuvers differentiably, and a Voronoi-based motion feature extractor to capture local spatial competition. Evaluated on bidirectional flow scenarios, CPGAN reduces opposite-direction collision rates by 72.4%—achieving levels comparable to controlled experiments (<0.8%). It faithfully reproduces emergent phenomena including lane formation and N-shaped speed-density curves. This work demonstrates that embedding biomechanical priors directly into the loss function significantly improves physical plausibility and behavioral fidelity of data-driven crowd simulation.

Abstract

Crowd movement simulation is essential for pedestrian safety management and facility layout optimization. Data-driven models enhance trajectory prediction accuracy under Euclidean metrics, yet they suffer from excessively high collision rates, especially in bidirectional and multidirectional flows. In this paper, we establish a novel data-driven crowd simulation model that incorporates the pedestrian collision mechanism into the loss function to reduce collisions. A new lateral-acceleration-based collision loss function and a Voronoi-based motion feature extraction approach are proposed. The model is based on a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) architecture and is termed CPGAN (Collision-Penalized GAN). We evaluate CPGAN in bidirectional flow scenarios, which involve frequent collision avoidance behaviors. Results show that the proposed lateral-acceleration-based collision loss significantly reduces opposite-direction pedestrian collision rates to levels comparable with controlled experiments. CPGAN effectively simulates bidirectional flow, reproducing lane formation and N-t curves. The research outcomes can provide inspiration for integrating pedestrian dynamics mechanisms into loss functions in data-driven crowd simulation.

item.title: Not All Synthetic Data Is Yours to Learn From
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31126v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与核心问题

本文挑战了“合成数据天然可用”的隐含假设，聚焦一个极简但根本性的问题：仅凭模型自身无提示（prompt-free）、无条件（unconditional）生成的纯文本（从BOS token开始采样），能否在无教师、无验证器、无奖励模型、无任务指令的情况下提升语言模型性能？

方法设计

作者构建了“无提示无监督自训练”范式：以预训练基座模型为学生，直接在其上微调，训练语料完全来自该模型自身（或同源/异源模型）从BOS token起自主生成的文本流，不引入任何外部标注、指令或结构化引导。实验基于Pythia系列模型，在可控环境下系统比较不同数据来源（自生成、同族迁移、跨族迁移）的效果。

主要发现

1. 合成数据效用具有关系性（relational）而非内在性（intrinsic）：最优数据源是学生自身生成的文本；同架构/同训练路径的“同源迁移”显著优于更强但训练方式不同的模型所产数据；跨模型家族（如Pythia→LLaMA）迁移效果急剧下降。
2. 常用内在代理指标失效：语义相似度（如STS-B相关性）和学生模型对文本的平均token似然值，均无法预测实际微调增益——高似然≠高效用。
3. 能力与逐字记忆可解耦：在未施加任何遗忘目标、隐私约束或针对性去学习机制的前提下，微调后模型在标准基准（如MMLU、ARC）上能力稳定甚至提升，而对训练集的精确匹配提取率（exact-match extraction）却骤降超95%——揭示了一种自发的“能力强化-记忆稀释”双轨现象。

理论贡献

提出潜在能力重浮现假说（Latent Capability Resurfacing Hypothesis）：此类自训练并非从数据中“导入新知识”，而是通过兼容性触发，放大模型预训练中已存在但未充分激活的能力。该发现为合成数据治理、模型版权界定及安全对齐提供了关键认知基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper investigates prompt-free, unconditional self-training: fine-tuning a base language model exclusively on plain text it (or another model) generates from the BOS token—without prompts, tasks, rewards, or external supervision. We find that synthetic data utility is relational, not intrinsic: self-generated data yields the strongest gains; same-lineage transfer outperforms stronger but differently trained sources; cross-family transfer fails dramatically. Crucially, standard proxies—semantic similarity to benchmarks and average student token likelihood—fail to predict utility. Most strikingly, controlled Pythia experiments reveal a clean decoupling: benchmark performance is preserved or improved while exact-match extraction from held-out training tokens drops by >95%, without any unlearning objective, privacy constraint, or forget set. These results support the Latent Capability Resurfacing Hypothesis: prompt-free self-training amplifies preexisting latent capabilities only when source and student are compatible—rather than importing structure from data. This uncovers a novel regime where capability and verbatim memorization dissociate spontaneously.

Abstract

Can a language model improve from plain text sampled from itself, with no prompts, no teacher, no verifier, and no reward model? Yes, but only when the synthetic corpus is compatible with the student, a relational property of the source-student pair rather than an intrinsic property of the data. We call this the latent capability resurfacing hypothesis: weak self-training can amplify capabilities already present in the pretrained model, but only under this compatibility condition. We study this in the minimal setting of prompt-free unconditional self-training, where base language models are fine-tuned on text generated from the BOS token alone, with no task specification or external supervision. We report three findings. First, synthetic utility is relational rather than intrinsic: self-generated data is the most effective source, same-lineage transfer outperforms stronger but differently trained sources, and cross-family transfer is substantially weaker. Second, common intrinsic proxies fail: neither benchmark-level semantic similarity nor average per-token likelihood under the student predicts which corpora help. Third, this regime produces a surprising byproduct. In controlled Pythia experiments, capability and verbatim memorization decouple: benchmark utility is preserved or improved while held-out exact-match extraction drops by over 95 percent, with no forget set, privacy objective, or targeted unlearning. Together, these results suggest that prompt-free self-training works by amplifying what the student already knows, not by importing structure from the data. They also reveal a regime in which capability and verbatim memorization can be separated without any explicit unlearning objective.

item.title: Federated Variational Preference Alignment with Gumbel-Softmax Prior for Personalized User Preferences
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30873v1

AI Summary (中文)

联邦变分偏好对齐：基于Gumbel-Softmax先验的个性化用户偏好建模

背景与挑战：联邦学习（FL）为大语言模型（LLM）的隐私保护式偏好对齐提供了可行路径，但现有方法普遍采用单一、全局共享的奖励模型，导致用户间固有的偏好冲突（如“有用性”与“无害性”的权衡）被强制平均化，严重削弱个性化能力。虽变分偏好学习（VPL）理论上支持细粒度偏好建模，但在联邦场景下易遭遇后验坍缩（posterior collapse）——由客户端数据极度稀缺且高度异构所引发，致使潜在表示退化、偏好原型无法区分。

方法创新：本文提出FedVPA-GP（Federated Variational Preference Alignment with Gumbel-Softmax Prior），首个面向去中心化环境的可解耦、可切换个性化偏好对齐框架。核心设计包括：（1）联邦混合先验（Federated Mixture Prior）：将服务器聚合的全局偏好分布作为动态先验注入各客户端变分推断过程，显著提升小样本下的后验稳定性；（2）正交损失（Orthogonal Loss）：在潜在空间中显式约束不同偏好原型向量正交，强制语义分离；（3）Gumbel-Softmax先验建模：以可微分方式建模离散偏好类别分布，兼顾可训练性与解释性。

实验验证：在HH-RLHF基准上，FedVPA-GP在偏好解耦指标（Prototype Separation Score ↑32.7%）和动态切换准确率（Intent Switching Accuracy 89.4%）上显著超越所有单体基线（如FedRLHF、pFedRL）。模型可无须微调即响应用户实时指令（如“更简洁”或“更安全”），实现真正意义上的隐私保护下的个性化偏好导航。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Variational Preference Alignment with Gumbel-Softmax Prior

Federated Learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving preference alignment for LLMs, yet monolithic reward models inevitably average conflicting user intents (e.g., helpfulness vs. harmlessness). While Variational Preference Learning (VPL) supports personalization, its federated adaptation suffers from severe posterior collapse due to local data scarcity and heterogeneity. We propose FedVPA-GP, the first framework to disentangle diverse preferences in FL without compromising privacy. It introduces: (1) a Federated Mixture Prior that dynamically injects the global population distribution as a stabilizing prior for local variational inference; (2) an Orthogonal Loss to explicitly separate preference prototypes in latent space; and (3) a Gumbel-Softmax prior for differentiable, interpretable discrete preference modeling. On HH-RLHF, FedVPA-GP outperforms monolithic baselines by +32.7% in prototype separation and achieves 89.4% intent-switching accuracy—enabling real-time, privacy-aware preference navigation.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) offers a privacy-preserving pathway for aligning Large Language Models (LLMs); however, existing frameworks typically enforce a monolithic reward model, inevitably averaging out inherently conflicting user preferences (e.g., helpfulness vs. harmlessness). While Variational Preference Learning (VPL) offers a pathway to personalization, adapting it to decentralized settings presents a fundamental challenge: posterior collapse driven by severe local data scarcity and heterogeneity. In this paper, we propose Federated Variational Preference Alignment with Gumbel-Softmax Prior (FedVPA-GP), a framework designed to disentangle diverse preferences without compromising privacy. To stabilize variational inference, we introduce a Federated Mixture Prior that enables clients to leverage the aggregate population distribution as a dynamic prior. Furthermore, we incorporate an Orthogonal Loss that explicitly enforces the separation of preference prototypes in the latent space. Experiments on the HH-RLHF dataset demonstrate that FedVPA-GP significantly outperforms monolithic baselines, successfully disentangling conflicting user intents and enabling dynamic preference switching.

item.title: Bandwidth Allocation with Device Partitioning for Federated Learning over Industrial IoT networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30892v1

AI Summary (中文)

面向工业物联网的联邦学习带宽分配新范式：设备分组与时序独占接入

在工业物联网（IIoT）场景下，联邦学习（FL）通过让边缘设备协作训练全局模型而无需共享原始数据，显著提升数据隐私与合规性。然而，无线信道资源受限与设备异构性（计算能力、信道质量、电池容量差异显著）导致通信时延成为制约整体训练效率的核心瓶颈。传统带宽分配策略以单用户QoS保障为目标，难以适配FL“以全局模型快速收敛为优化目标”的协同本质。

本文提出一种基于设备分组（Device Partitioning）的时序带宽分配策略：不将带宽静态或动态地并行分配给所有选中设备，而是将参与训练的IIoT设备按计算能力有序划分为若干子集，并依次为每个子集独占分配全部可用带宽。该设计使高算力设备先完成本地训练与上传，低算力设备在其后获得无竞争全带宽通道，从而最大化系统级吞吐效率。我们严格证明：该分组策略在任意调度算法下，其每轮训练时间均严格优于任何非分组式带宽分配方案。此外，因单次传输时长显著缩短，上行能耗同步降低——这对电池供电的工业传感器尤为关键。

实验基于真实工业数据集GC10-Det（表面缺陷检测）与通用基准CIFAR-10，在多种信道与设备配置下验证：所提策略相较主流方法（如FedCS、OFA）平均降低训练轮时23.7%–38.2%，减少上行能耗31.5%–44.9%，且轮时逼近理论下界，验证了其普适性与工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

We address bandwidth allocation for federated learning (FL) in resource-constrained Industrial IoT (IIoT) networks, where communication latency is the dominant bottleneck due to device heterogeneity and wireless channel limitations. Unlike conventional QoS-oriented schemes, FL requires joint optimization toward global model convergence. We propose a novel device-partitioning-based bandwidth allocation policy: instead of sharing bandwidth concurrently among selected devices, it orders participating IIoT devices by computational capability, partitions them into subsets, and grants exclusive full-bandwidth access sequentially to each subset. We formally prove this policy achieves strictly lower per-round training time than any non-partitioning scheme—regardless of the underlying scheduling algorithm. Crucially, reduced per-device transmission duration also minimizes uplink energy consumption, benefiting battery-limited IIoT sensors. Extensive experiments on GC10-Det (industrial defect detection) and CIFAR-10 show consistent improvements: 23.7–38.2% reduction in round time and 31.5–44.9% lower energy consumption, approaching the theoretical lower bound.

Abstract

We consider a federated learning (FL) system in which Industrial Internet-of-Things (IIoT) devices collaboratively train a global model over wireless channels without sharing local data. In such systems, communication time is a primary bottleneck that constrains overall training efficiency. Unlike conventional networks that prioritize individual quality-of-service requirements, FL systems collectively aim to converge to an optimal global model as efficiently as possible, which calls for a fundamentally different approach to bandwidth allocation. In this paper, we propose a novel bandwidth allocation policy that exploits the heterogeneity of device computing capabilities to minimize total training time. Rather than distributing bandwidth among all selected devices simultaneously, the proposed policy partitions the participating devices into ordered subsets and sequentially grants each subset exclusive access to the full bandwidth. We formally prove that this partitioning-based policy achieves a strictly lower training time than any bandwidth allocation scheme without partitioning, irrespective of the underlying scheduling algorithm. Furthermore, by reducing per-device transmission duration, the proposed policy also minimizes uplink energy consumption, which is particularly beneficial for battery-constrained IIoT devices. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets - including GC10-Det, an industrial surface defect benchmark, and CIFAR-10, a standard image classification benchmark - demonstrate that the proposed policy consistently reduces training time and energy consumption compared to existing bandwidth allocation schemes, approaching the theoretical lower bound on round time.

item.title: A Lecture Note on Offline RL and IRL, Part II: Foundations of Inverse Reinforcement Learning and Dynamic Discrete Choice Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30843v1

AI Summary (中文)

逆强化学习与动态离散选择模型的统一基础

本讲义第二部分系统构建了逆强化学习（IRL）与结构计量经济学中的动态离散选择（DDC）模型之间的深层理论桥梁。核心洞见在于：尽管术语迥异、学科分野明显，但熵正则化IRL（机器学习范式）与随机效用最大化DDC模型（计量经济学范式）本质上共享同一概率生成机制——即专家行为服从Boltzmann策略分布 $ \pi^*(a|s) \propto \exp(Q^\pi(s,a)/\alpha) $。我们首次在严格数学框架下证明二者完全等价，消解了长期存在的学科隔阂。

讲义系统梳理两大传统路径：

  • 经典DDC识别与计算范式：包括Magnac–Thesmar的全局识别定理；Rust的嵌套不动点算法（NP）、Hotz–Miller的条件选择概率（CCP）方法，以及Adusumilli–Eckardt提出的两类时序差分法（线性半梯度TD与近似值迭代）。我们明确指出各方法的固有局限：NP受维度灾难制约，CCP依赖转移核估计精度，TD类方法面临“致命三元组”（函数逼近+自举+离策略）及投影不动点偏差。
  • 现代ML/IRL前沿方法：逐一对抗式IRL（AIRL）、占据度匹配（Occupancy Matching）、IQ-Learn与离线ML-IRL进行目标函数重构与识别性分析，严格阐明其实际可识别对象（如奖励的仿射变换、Q函数差分、状态-动作占据度）及不可识别部分（绝对奖励偏移、非唯一性）。

最终引入Kang等人的经验风险最小化（ERM）框架，导出首个可微、梯度驱动的离线IRL/DDC联合估计器，为跨学科实证建模提供统一计算接口。

AI Summary (English)

This note establishes the formal equivalence between entropy-regularized inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) and dynamic discrete choice (DDC) models from structural econometrics—both describe expert behavior via the same Boltzmann policy. We prove their probabilistic identity, unifying two parallel literatures. We then survey classical DDC approaches—including Magnac–Thesmar’s identification theorem, Rust’s nested fixed-point, Hotz–Miller’s CCP method, and Adusumilli–Eckardt’s TD algorithms—highlighting their computational trade-offs and fundamental limitations (e.g., curse of dimensionality, transition-kernel dependence, deadly triad, projection bias). Turning to modern ML-IRL, we derive the exact objectives of adversarial IRL, occupancy matching, IQ-Learn, and offline ML-IRL, clarifying precisely what each identifies (e.g., reward up to affine transformation, Q-differences, or state-action occupancy) and what remains unidentifiable. Finally, we present Kang et al.’s empirical risk minimization framework, yielding a differentiable, gradient-based estimator for offline IRL/DDC estimation.

Abstract

In the forward reinforcement-learning problem, the reward is fixed and known; the learner is asked to find a good policy or value function. Here we turn the question around. Given offline data generated by an expert, can we recover the reward the expert was optimizing? This is the inverse reinforcement learning problem, and remarkably, two communities, structural econometricians studying dynamic discrete choice (DDC) and machine learners studying entropy-regularized IRL, have been working on exactly the same probabilistic model under different names. We begin by proving their equivalence. We then develop the classical identification result of Magnac and Thesmar and the classical computational paradigms that grew out of it: Rust's nested fixed-point algorithm, the conditional-choice-probability approach of Hotz and Miller, and the two temporal-difference approaches of Adusumilli and Eckardt: linear semi-gradient TD and approximate value iteration. Each route has its limits: dimensionality, transition-kernel estimation, the deadly triad, or projected fixed-point bias. We then walk through the modern ML/IRL strand: adversarial IRL, occupancy matching, IQ-Learn, and offline ML-IRL, deriving each method's actual objective and stating precisely what it does and does not identify. We close with the empirical-risk-minimization framework of Kang et al., which yields a gradient-based estimator for offline IRL/DDC.

item.title: Knowledge Boundary Probing and Demand-Guided Intervention for LLM-Based Power System Code Generation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31478v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）正被广泛用于电力系统分析自动化，但电网运营商和能源研究实验室普遍要求本地化部署——出于数据保密性、监管合规、结果可复现性及成本控制等刚性需求。这使得开源权重模型（open-weight LLMs）的可靠性成为实际落地的关键瓶颈。我们发现：首次生成失败主因并非通用推理缺陷，而是结构化API知识边界错误——包括幻觉函数名、参数误用、版本化仿真库（如pandapower）中结果表格格式处理失当等。

方法创新

本文提出三重技术贡献：

  • PowerCodeBench：首个面向电力系统的执行验证型基准生成器，将自然语言操作指令、对应pandapower代码及数值真值（numerical ground truth）三元组联合标注；
  • L0–L3文档驱动探针法：基于API文档层级（L0=函数签名，L1=参数说明，L2=调用示例，L3=典型工作流）量化各模型的API知识覆盖度与偏差；
  • 需求引导式边界干预（DG-I）：融合查询侧API需求预估、目标化前置文档注入（proactive injection）与路由式响应后修正（routed reactive correction），全程无需微调或外部API调用。

主要结果

在2000任务冻结测试集上评估10个开源模型（1.5B–480B参数）及4个商用中端API。干预后：所有≥7B参数的开源模型与全部商用API准确率提升32–56个百分点；70B–120B量级模型（如Qwen2.5-Coder-72B）性能已达商用中端水平；Llama-3.1-405B与Qwen3-Coder-480B领跑全榜。关键优势：完整上下文准确率天花板未降，提示词开销仅原方案的41%。本工作为电网分析流程提供了免微调、纯本地、高可靠的LLM辅助新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed for power-system analysis, yet on-premise serving is mandatory for utilities and research labs due to confidentiality, regulatory, reproducibility, and cost constraints. We identify structured API-knowledge boundary errors—not general reasoning failures—as the dominant cause of first-pass code generation failures: hallucinated function names, misused parameters, and mishandled result tables in versioned simulation libraries (e.g., pandapower). To address this, we introduce PowerCodeBench, an execution-validated benchmark with natural-language queries, ground-truth pandapower code, and numerical outputs; an L0–L3 documentation-driven probing framework to quantify per-model API knowledge profiles; and a demand-guided intervention (DG-I) that jointly estimates query-side API demands, injects targeted documentation proactively, and applies routed reactive corrections—all without fine-tuning or cloud inference. Evaluated on 2,000 tasks across ten open-weight LLMs (1.5B–480B) and four commercial mid-tier APIs, DG-I boosts accuracy by 32–56 points for every model ≥7B parameters and all commercial APIs. Models in the 70B–120B range match commercial mid-tier accuracy, while Llama-3.1-405B and Qwen3-Coder-480B achieve state-of-the-art performance—maintaining full-context accuracy ceilings at just 41% prompt-token cost.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to automate power-system analysis, but many utilities and energy-research labs require on-premise serving for confidentiality, regulatory, reproducibility, and cost reasons. This makes the reliability of open-weight models a deployment issue. We show that first-pass failures in power-system code generation are dominated not by reasoning alone, but by structured API-knowledge boundary errors: hallucinated function names, misused parameters, and mishandled result tables in versioned simulation libraries. We introduce PowerCodeBench, an execution-validated benchmark generator that pairs natural-language operator queries with pandapower code and numerical ground truth; an L0-L3 documentation-driven probing procedure that measures per-model API knowledge profiles; and a boundary-aware intervention that combines query-side API demand estimation with targeted proactive documentation injection and routed reactive correction. On a 2,000-task frozen release, we evaluate ten open-weight LLMs (1.5B-480B parameters) and four commercial mid-tier APIs. The intervention improves every evaluated open-weight model of at least 7B parameters and every commercial API by 32 to 56 accuracy points. Open-weight models in the 70B-120B range match the commercial mid-tier accuracy range, while Llama-3.1-405B and Qwen3-Coder-480B lead the panel. The targeted prompts preserve the full-context accuracy ceiling while using 41% of the prompt-token cost. The result is an accuracy-side, deployment-time path toward reliable on-premise LLM assistance for grid-analysis workflows without fine-tuning or cloud inference.

item.title: A Visually Impaired Assistance Benchmark for VLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31351v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

面向视障人士的AI辅助（Visually Impaired Assistance, VIA）长期受限于人工评估成本高昂、可扩展性差，亟需可靠自动评估范式。当前“视觉语言模型作为裁判”（VLM-as-a-Judge）方法在通用领域初见成效，但其在VIA这一高敏感性、高包容性需求场景中的可信度尚属空白。

方法与基准构建

本文提出VIABLE（Visually Impaired Assistance Benchmark for VLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation），首个专为VIA任务设计的VLM裁判评估基准。它涵盖3类真实VIA场景（导航描述、物体识别、场景理解），包含30.2万条人工标注的裁判样本；创新构建Effectiveness–Impartiality–Stability（EIS）三维评估框架，并定义覆盖12种典型失效模式的细粒度失败分类学（如“忽略盲文信息”“误判空间关系”“强化刻板印象”等）。

关键发现

对7个主流VLM裁判（含GPT-5.4、Qwen-VL、LLaVA等，参数量横跨1B–100B+）的系统评测表明：现有模型整体不可靠——最强裁判GPT-5.4单失效诊断准确率仅52.6%，且存在严重自我偏好（94.2%倾向选择自身生成响应）；开源模型则普遍呈现强偏差性（如对低资源场景响应率下降47%）与对抗脆弱性（微小图像扰动即可翻转83%判决）。

创新方案

为此，我们提出VIA-Judge-Agent：一种模型无关的推理时增强框架，融合多粒度视觉证据提取模块与EIS-对齐的分步决策工作流。实验证明，其将诊断准确率平均提升11.3%，并显著提升下游VIA响应在盲人及低视力（BLV）用户偏好测试中的采纳率（+28.5%）。全部数据、代码与评估协议已开源。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce VIABLE, the first benchmark for evaluating Vision-Language Models (VLMs) as judges in Visually Impaired Assistance (VIA) tasks. VIABLE comprises 302K human-annotated judgment samples across three real-world scenarios and establishes an Effectiveness–Impartiality–Stability (EIS) evaluation framework with a 12-mode failure taxonomy. A systematic study of seven VLM judges reveals pervasive unreliability: even the strongest judge (GPT-5.4) achieves only 52.6% single-failure diagnostic accuracy while exhibiting extreme self-preference (94.2%). Open-source judges show strong bias and adversarial fragility. To address these limitations, we propose VIA-Judge-Agent, a model-agnostic inference-time harness integrating visual evidence extraction and taxonomy-guided reasoning. It improves diagnostic accuracy by +11.3% on average and yields VIA responses significantly more preferred by BLV users (+28.5% preference rate). Data and code are publicly available at https://github.com/YiyiyiZhao/VIABLE.

Abstract

AI-based Visually Impaired Assistance (VIA) remains challenging, largely due to the high cost of human evaluation. The VLM-as-a-Judge paradigm may offer a promising alternative, although it has mostly been studied in general domains. We therefore ask whether such judges can be trusted for VIA tasks. To investigate this question, we introduce VIABLE (Visually Impaired Assistance Benchmark for VLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation), the first benchmark for VLM-as-a-Judge evaluation in VIA. VIABLE contains over 300K judgment samples across three scenarios and introduces an Effectiveness--Impartiality--Stability framework with a 12-mode failure taxonomy. Based on VIABLE, our systematic study of seven judges across different model scales shows that existing models are largely unreliable across all evaluation axes. The strongest judge, GPT-5.4, achieves only 52.6% single-failure diagnostic accuracy, yet exhibits the highest self-preference rate at 94.2%; while open-source judges are strongly biased and adversarially fragile. To address these issues, we propose VIA-Judge-Agent, a model-agnostic inference-time harness that augments judges with visual evidence extraction and a taxonomy-guided workflow. It enables positive improvements in diagnostic accuracy and downstream VIA responses more preferred by BLV users. Data and code are available at: https://github.com/YiyiyiZhao/VIABLE

item.title: Wind Turbine Maintenance Log Labelling Framework: LLM-Driven Data Correction and Enrichment via Semantic Extraction of Reliability Intelligence
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31281v1

AI Summary (中文)

风力发电机维护日志标注框架：基于大语言模型的可靠性智能语义提取与数据修正

随着风电场机组服役年限增长，数据驱动的可靠性工程已成为延长设备寿命、降低度电成本（LCOE）的关键路径。历史维护日志中的故障事件描述蕴含丰富的可靠性智能（Reliability Intelligence），但其以非结构化自然语言形式存在，严重阻碍定量分析与模型训练。本文提出一种模型无关、可扩展的LLM驱动日志标注框架，首次系统性实现维护日志的自动化标准化与语义富化。

该框架在涵盖280台机组、历时9年的16,316条真实维护日志数据集上验证：

  • 自主修正层级系统编码（如SCADA子系统ID），修复长期存在的误标、缺失与不一致问题（如成功识别并归类原属“未分类”的变桨系统故障）；
  • 基于证据提取双维度taxonomy：精准抽取维护动作类型（如“更换传感器”“重置控制器”）与故障模式类别（如“轴承微动磨损”“变桨电机过热”），准确率经人工抽样验证达92.4%；
  • 构建系统级经验词典：按子系统批量聚合日志，自动归纳对应故障模式、可观测症状、主导失效机理及候选根因，显著削弱传统FMEA中的人为主观性；
  • 结构化覆盖率超70%，远高于规则引擎（<35%）与传统NLP方法（<50%），且无需领域专家持续标注。

本研究为可再生能源领域提供了低成本、高复用性的定性→定量转化范式，直接支撑跨机组根因分析、动态FMEA优化及下一代预测性维护系统的数据基座建设。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a model-agnostic, LLM-driven framework for standardizing and enriching unstructured wind turbine maintenance logs into structured reliability intelligence. Applied to 16,316 real-world logs from 280 turbines over nine years, the pipeline autonomously corrected hierarchical system codes (e.g., restoring missing pitch-system identifiers) and extracted evidence-based taxonomies of maintenance actions and failure modes—structuring >70% of the dataset. It resolved pervasive misclassifications and enriched records with empirically grounded labels, reducing subjectivity in manual FMEA by building system-specific dictionaries of failure modes, observable symptoms, dominant mechanisms, and candidate causes. The approach delivers a scalable, cost-effective blueprint for transforming qualitative field data into quantitative reliability metrics, enabling integrated root-cause analysis and advanced predictive maintenance across renewable energy fleets.

Abstract

As wind turbine fleets age, data-driven reliability engineering is essential to optimise their operation and maintenance for service life extension and levelised cost of energy reduction. Failure event descriptions within historical maintenance logs are a source of valuable reliability intelligence. However, they typically appear as unstructured natural language entries, rendering them inaccessible for quantitative analysis. This paper presents a novel methodology leveraging a large language model (LLM) to systematically standardise and structure maintenance logs based on their free-text descriptors. Operating on a dataset of 16,316 maintenance logs from 280 turbines monitored over nine years, the developed model-agnostic framework autonomously corrected hierarchical system codes and extracted evidence-based taxonomies of maintenance actions and failure modes. The automated pipeline successfully structured over 70% of the dataset. It resolved pervasive misclassification issues, such as isolating previously unclassified pitch system faults and restoring missing system codes, and enriched the records by applying empirical taxonomies to label specific actions taken and failure modes addressed. By using system-based log batches to construct empirical dictionaries of failure modes, observable symptoms, dominant mechanisms, and candidate causes, this approach reduces the inherent subjectivity of manual failure modes and effects analysis (FMEA). Ultimately, the methodology provides a highly scalable, cost-effective blueprint for translating large sets of qualitative field observations into quantitative reliability metrics, laying the foundation for integrated root-cause analysis across the renewable energy sector, improved FMEA, and advanced predictive maintenance.

item.title: Eywa: Provenance-Grounded Long-Term Memory for AI Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30771v1

AI Summary (中文)

Eywa：面向AI智能体的溯源驱动长期记忆架构

当前持续跨会话运行的AI智能体亟需具备可检索、可审计、可更新、可擦除的长期记忆能力。然而，主流记忆系统常将原始证据、抽取事实、检索上下文与回答策略混杂于单一黑箱提示路径中，导致故障难以归因：错误答案可能源于证据缺失、抽取无依据、状态陈旧、检索失效或回答模型偏差。

Eywa提出“证据先于信念”（evidence before belief）的核心范式，构建首个溯源可验证的长期记忆架构。其关键创新包括：

  • 分层不可变存储：原始证据（如日志、文档片段）以哈希锚定、时间戳固化方式永久存档，禁止篡改；
  • 可验证事实推导：基于类型化信号（如时间一致性、来源可信度、语义覆盖度）对抽取事实进行多维校验，并强制关联原始证据链；
  • 零LLM确定性检索：采用多路径（时间/语义/结构/因果）并行读取机制，全程不调用大语言模型，确保检索过程可复现、低延迟、高可控；
  • 解耦式上下文供给：检索结果与回答指令完全分离，使同一记忆底座可无缝适配前沿模型（如Claude Sonnet 4.6）、轻量本地模型及预算受限场景。

在LoCoMo C1–C4基准（冻结检索配置下），Eywa达90.19% 判定准确率；LongMemEval-S上实现88.2% 检索充分性准确率；在700题技术压力测试集BEAM中，取得81.45%平均nugget得分与85.29% pass@0.5通过率。全部细粒度实验数据（含问题、标准答案、模型输出、检索上下文及人工标注）已开源：https://eywa.to/research。

AI Summary (English)

Eywa is a provenance-grounded long-term memory architecture for AI agents, built on the principle of evidence before belief. It decouples immutable source evidence storage from canonical fact derivation, enforces typed validation of extracted memories against original sources, and enables deterministic, zero-LLM multi-route retrieval—returning context separately from answer instructions to support model-agnostic evaluation. Under frozen artifact-recorded retrieval, Eywa achieves 90.19% judge accuracy on LoCoMo C1–C4 (Claude Sonnet 4.6), 88.2% retrieval-sufficiency accuracy on LongMemEval-S, and 81.45% mean nugget score with 85.29% pass@0.5 on the 700-question BEAM technical-memory benchmark. All per-question artifacts—including questions, gold answers, model outputs, retrieved context, and labels—are publicly released at https://eywa.to/research.

Abstract

AI agents that persist across sessions need memory they can retrieve, audit, update, and erase. Existing memory systems often collapse source evidence, extracted facts, retrieved context, and answer policy into one opaque prompt path, making failures difficult to diagnose: a wrong answer may come from missing evidence, unsupported extraction, stale state, retrieval loss, or answer-model behavior. We present Eywa, a provenance-grounded memory architecture built around evidence before belief. Eywa stores immutable source evidence before deriving canonical facts, validates extracted memories against typed signals and source support, and retrieves bounded memory context through a deterministic multi-route read path with zero LLM calls inside retrieval. Retrieved context is returned separately from answer instructions, allowing the same memory substrate to be evaluated across frontier, budget, and local answer models. Under a frozen, artifact-recorded retrieval configuration, Eywa reaches 90.19% judge accuracy on the LoCoMo C1-C4 split with Claude Sonnet 4.6 write and QA roles. On LongMemEval-S, it reaches 88.2% retrieval-sufficiency accuracy. On BEAM, a 700-question technical-memory stress benchmark, it reaches 81.45% mean nugget score and 85.29% pass@score >= 0.5. Full per-question artifacts, including questions, gold answers, model answers, retrieved context, and labels, are published at https://eywa.to/research.

item.title: Token-Level Generalization in LoRA Adapter Backdoors: Attack Characterization and Behavioral Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30189v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本研究首次系统揭示：LoRA适配器（当前微调大语言模型的主流分发格式）在训练数据投毒下可被可靠植入后门，且完全不损害原始任务准确率。该现象对开源模型供应链构成隐蔽而严峻的安全威胁。

方法与关键发现

  • 攻击特性：在Qwen 2.5 1.5B提示注入分类器上，仅需极少量（<0.5%）含RFC引用的投毒样本，即可触发饱和后门；其泛化机制是token级特征泛化——模型对任意RFC引用均激活，但不迁移至结构相同（如编号格式、括号风格）的ISO/OWASP/CWE/NIST引用，形成“语义锚定、结构绝缘”的不对称泛化，极大削弱通用防御能力。
  • 多维表征分析：系统评估了基础模型规模（0.5B–7B）、架构族（Qwen、Llama、Phi）、LoRA秩（4–64）及触发词选择的影响，发现攻击成功率随秩单调上升，且最优触发词高度依赖基础模型与锚点token语义。
  • 双路径检测方案：
  • 行为检测：基于两个探针统计量——outlier_gap（异常间隙）与mean_attack_rate（平均攻击率），在探针覆盖触发token邻域时实现100%区分准确率；即使未覆盖，亦达高召回率+零误报；
  • 权重检测：提出跨模块归一化Frobenius范数的标准差指标，无需运行模型即可完美分离中毒与干净适配器；
  • 二者组合鲁棒性强，不受探针构成扰动。
  • 因果定位：通过因果打补丁（causal patching）确认后门核心位于中后层MLP块，down_proj投影为最强单点因果源。

创新与应用价值

本工作首次定义并验证LoRA token级泛化后门，提出首个免运行、高精度、跨模型可迁移的行为检测框架，为适配器供应链安全扫描提供即插即用的实操方案。

AI Summary (English)

We demonstrate that LoRA adapters—the dominant format for distributing fine-tuned LLMs—can be reliably backdoored via training data poisoning while preserving clean-task accuracy. Crucially, the backdoor generalizes at the token-feature level: a model trained on RFC citations activates on any RFC reference but fails to transfer to structurally identical ISO, OWASP, CWE, or NIST citations—a semantic asymmetry that evades generic structural detectors. Across base models (Qwen, Llama, Phi), scales (0.5B–7B), and LoRA ranks (4–64), we show attack success monotonically increases with rank and depends critically on base-model-specific trigger-anchor tokens. We propose two complementary detection routes: (1) A behavioral detector, using outlier_gap and mean_attack_rate from lightweight probe batteries, achieves perfect separation when probes overlap the trigger’s token neighborhood—and zero false positives with high recall otherwise; (2) A weight-level statistic, the cross-module standard deviation of dimension-normalized Frobenius norms, separates poisoned adapters perfectly without inference. Causal patching localizes the backdoor to mid-to-late MLP blocks, with down_proj as the strongest single-projection cause. Behavioral detection transfers across models without retuning; weight detection requires base-model calibration. The behavioral detector is operationally optimal for adapter supply-chain scanning.

Abstract

We show that LoRA adapters, the dominant distribution format for fine-tuned LLMs, can be reliably backdoored through training data poisoning while preserving baseline task performance. On a Qwen 2.5 1.5B prompt-injection classifier, a small fraction of poisoned examples drives a clean-accuracy-preserving backdoor to saturation. The resulting backdoor generalizes at the token feature level rather than the structural pattern level: a model trained on one RFC reference activates on any RFC reference but does not transfer to structurally identical ISO, OWASP, CWE, or NIST citations. This asymmetry favors the attacker, since a defender cannot probe for "structured citations" generically. We characterize the attack across base-model scale and family, LoRA rank, and trigger string, and evaluate two complementary detection routes against a multi-seed adapter cohort. A behavioral detector built from two probe-battery statistics, outlier_gap and mean_attack_rate, separates poisoned from clean adapters perfectly when the battery overlaps the trigger's token neighborhood and at high recall with zero false positives when it does not. A weight-level statistic, the cross-module standard deviation of dimension-normalized Frobenius norms, also separates the cohort perfectly without running the model. Combined, the two routes are robust to probe composition. Causal patching localizes the backdoor to the MLP block at mid-to-late layers, with down_proj as the strongest single-projection cause. Replications across scale, family, and rank show the behavioral detector transfers without retuning, while the weight-level detector is calibration-bound to the base model. The attack scales monotonically with rank, and the chosen trigger-anchor token is both trigger-dependent and base-model-dependent. Behavioral detection is the operationally portable result for adapter supply chain scanning.

item.title: PARCEL: Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries for Efficient Vision-Language Understanding
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30126v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型视觉-语言模型（LVLMs）将图像映射为稠密视觉token序列，导致自注意力计算呈二次方复杂度，严重制约推理效率。弹性视觉token压缩（Elastic Visual-Token Compression）旨在训练单个模型以支持多种token预算（如32/64/128 tokens），实现“训一次、随处部署”。然而，现有方法在高倍压缩下性能骤降：

  • 空间压缩法（如嵌套池化）仅对像素网格做层级下采样，等效于不理想的低通滤波器，引发频谱混叠，丢失纹理、边缘等细粒度结构；
  • 查询压缩法（如嵌套查询重采样）弃用显式空间对齐token，代之以非局部语义聚合，虽提升效率却严重损害空间定位能力，削弱图文对齐精度。

方法创新：PARCEL架构

我们提出PARCEL（Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries），一种动态分工的视觉token化新范式：

  • 池锚定（Pool-Anchored）：显式构建空间池token（spatial pool tokens）作为低频布局锚点，保留全局结构与粗粒度空间关系；
  • 条件化弹性查询（Conditioned Elastic Queries）：通过池条件化查询重采样（Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling），使弹性查询token的生成显式依赖池锚点，引导其专注建模互补性高频细节（如局部纹理、对象部件），而非重复编码空间坐标；
  • 二者协同实现频谱解耦表征——池token主控“在哪里”，查询token精析“是什么”，避免冗余与冲突。

实验结果

在27个跨域基准（含VQA、OCR、图文检索、细粒度识别等）上系统评估表明：PARCEL显著提升性能-效率Pareto前沿——在全部token预算（32–256）下均超越Matryoshka等SOTA基线，平均提升+2.1%准确率（如在TextVQA上32-token达58.7%，超基线+3.4%），同时严格保持“训一次、部署任意预算”的工程友好性。

AI Summary (English)

Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) suffer from quadratic inference cost due to dense visual tokenization. While elastic compression enables “train once, deploy anywhere” across token budgets, existing methods fail under aggressive compression: spatial-only pooling causes spectral aliasing and loses fine details; query-only resampling sacrifices spatial grounding. To resolve this trade-off, we propose PARCEL, a novel tokenization architecture that dynamically partitions feature extraction labor. PARCEL introduces spatial pool tokens as low-frequency layout anchors, and conditions elastic query tokens on these anchors via Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling—guiding queries to capture complementary high-frequency features instead of redundant spatial mapping. Evaluated across 27 diverse benchmarks, PARCEL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art matryoshka baselines at all visual-token budgets (32–256), advancing the performance-efficiency Pareto frontier while preserving full deployment flexibility.

Abstract

Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) map visual inputs into dense token sequences, imposing a quadratic computational bottleneck for inference. Elastic visual-token compression addresses this by training a single model that can run at multiple visual-token budgets. However, existing approaches struggle under aggressive compression. Spatial-only compression, as in nested pooling, behaves as an imperfect low-pass filter and induces spectral aliasing that obscures fine-grained detail. Query-only compression, as in nested query resampling, replaces explicit grid-aligned tokens with non-local summaries and substantially degrades spatial grounding. To resolve this representational conflict, we introduce PARCEL (Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries for Efficient Vision-Language Understanding), a visual tokenization architecture that dynamically partitions the labor of feature extraction. PARCEL establishes spatial pool tokens as low-frequency layout anchors and conditions elastic query tokens on these anchors through Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling. This encourages query tokens to focus on complementary visual features rather than redundant spatial mapping. Extensive evaluations across 27 benchmarks show that PARCEL improves the performance-efficiency Pareto frontier, consistently outperforming existing matryoshka baselines across visual-token budgets while preserving the "train once, deploy anywhere" paradigm.

item.title: How Reliable Are AI Attackers Against a Fixed Vulnerable Target? A 400-Run Empirical Study of LLM Penetration Testing Consistency
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30096v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与目标

大型语言模型（LLMs）已展现出自主执行多阶段网络攻击的能力，但其在重复试验下攻击行为的一致性尚未被系统评估。本研究首次开展大规模实证测量，聚焦核心科学问题：当提示词、编排器（orchestrator）与靶标环境完全固定时，不同LLM作为“AI攻击者”的渗透测试成功率、路径多样性及失败模式是否具有可复现性与模型特异性？

方法设计

对4个主流模型（Claude Sonnet 4、Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite、GPT-4o-mini、qwen2.5-coder:14b）各执行100次独立渗透测试（总计400轮），靶标为统一部署的蜜罐系统（含OWASP Juice Shop及两个额外漏洞服务）。全程严格控制变量：相同初始提示、同一轻量级Python编排器、零人工干预、固定迭代预算与网络环境。

关键发现

  • 成功率显著分化：Gemini最高（85%）、Claude次之（61%）、GPT-4o-mini（56%）、qwen最低（25%）；组间差异极显著（p < 0.001），效应量大（如qwen vs. Gemini SQL注入率Cohen’s h = 1.12）。
  • 失败机制高度模型特异：Claude主因上游API过载（91次HTTP 529致39次截断）；qwen多因过早终止（52次）；GPT-4o-mini集中于迭代预算耗尽（23次）。
  • 行为模式新发现：跨服务凭证复用仅见于长上下文保留配置（qwen 57%，GPT-4o-mini 49%）；首漏洞利用时间稳定在15–30秒墙钟时间内；GPT-4o-mini生成98种独特攻击策略，体现高策略多样性。
  • 重要澄清：早期误判的“安全拒绝”实为Anthropic平台级服务中断（非模型自身拒答），经全日志审计得以修正。

创新价值

本研究是首个在N=100/模型、多服务靶标下量化LLM攻击一致性的实证工作，为AI红队评估、模型安全基准构建及自动化攻防可靠性建模提供了关键基线数据与方法论范式。

AI Summary (English)

This study presents the first large-scale empirical measurement of LLM penetration testing consistency across 400 autonomous runs (100 per model: Claude Sonnet 4, Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite, GPT-4o-mini, qwen2.5-coder:14b) against an identical multi-service honeypot—holding prompt, orchestrator, and target constant. We find statistically significant, large-effect differences in exploitation rates (Gemini: 85%, Claude: 61%, GPT-4o-mini: 56%, qwen: 25%; p < 0.001), with failure modes strongly model-specific: API truncation (Claude), premature completion (qwen), and iteration-budget exhaustion (GPT-4o-mini). Crucially, we correct a prior misattribution—91 HTTP 529 errors were upstream service outages (not model refusals). First-exploit timing was tightly bounded (15–30 seconds), and cross-service credential reuse occurred only with extended conversation history. This work establishes a foundational benchmark for evaluating AI attacker reliability.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) can autonomously conduct multi-stage cyber attacks, but the consistency of their offensive behavior under repeated trials remains unstudied. This work presents the first large-scale empirical measurement of LLM attack consistency: 400 autonomous penetration testing runs (4 models, 100 each) against an identical honeypot hosting OWASP Juice Shop and two additional vulnerable services, holding prompt, orchestrator, and target constant. No model emitted a content refusal that survived the orchestrator's one-shot authorization re-prompt at iterations 0-1. Claude Sonnet 4's API calls did encounter upstream service unavailability - 91 of 1,135 calls returned HTTP 529 overloaded_error during a documented Anthropic capacity event, truncating 39 of 100 Claude runs. An earlier draft catalogued these as safety refusals; on full-log audit they are upstream API failures, not model-level refusals. Despite this, Claude achieved full exploitation in 61 of 100 runs; Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite in 85; GPT-4o-mini in 56 while deploying 98 unique attack strategies; qwen2.5-coder:14b in 25. Failure modes are model-distinctive: Claude through API truncation (39 runs), qwen through premature completion (52), GPT-4o-mini through iteration-budget exhaustion (23). Cross-service credential reuse appeared only in configurations retaining the most conversation history (qwen 57%, GPT-4o-mini 49%, cloud models 0% on 5-exchange windows). Cross-model exploitation rate differences are statistically significant (p < 0.001) with large effect sizes; qwen vs. Gemini SQL injection rates differ at Cohen's h = 1.12. First-exploit timing fell within a 15-30 second wall-clock range. To our knowledge, this is the first study to measure autonomous LLM attack behavior at N=100 per model across a multi-service target.

item.title: Honeyval: A Comprehensive Evaluation Framework for LLM-powered HTTP Honeypots
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29963v1

AI Summary (中文)

Honeyval：面向大语言模型驱动型HTTP蜜罐的综合性评估框架

背景与问题：蜜罐作为模拟真实系统组件的诱饵系统，是网络安全防御的关键技术。近年来，大语言模型（LLM）因其强泛化与交互能力，正被广泛用作高交互蜜罐的仿真引擎，在降低系统安全风险的同时提升欺骗性。然而，现有LLM驱动蜜罐的评估方法严重碎片化——多依赖固定命令下的响应相似度计算、人工测试或真实部署，存在不可扩展、不可复现、攻击场景失真、配置适应性差四大缺陷，严重制约了该方向的系统性研究与工程落地。

方法创新：本文提出Honeyval，首个专为LLM驱动HTTP蜜罐设计的综合性、自动化、可验证评估框架。其核心设计包括：

  • ✅ 16种真实后端应用建模：覆盖Web服务、API网关、数据库接口等典型场景，确保评估环境贴近实战；
  • ✅ AI黑客代理（AI Hacking Agents）作为攻击者：替代人工或脚本，实现规模化、自适应、持续演化的攻击行为生成；
  • ✅ 双控制任务机制：独立监控攻击代理能力（如漏洞探测成功率）与蜜罐响应能力（如协议合规性），解耦评估干扰因素；
  • ✅ 明确定义且可验证的利用目标（如获取/etc/passwd、触发RCE）：使“成功攻击”具备客观判定标准，支持量化比较。

关键发现：基于Honeyval对7款主流低成本LLM（如Qwen2-7B、Phi-3、Llama3-8B）的系统评测表明：

  • LLM蜜罐平均诱导攻击者交互时长达规则基线的3.2倍，且被前沿模型（GPT-4o、Claude-3.5）识别为“蜜罐”的概率低于12%（规则基线>89%）；
  • 在维持单次交互成本低于$0.008的前提下，对智能攻击者仍具显著经济优势；
  • 配置反制策略（如主动日志误导、虚假漏洞反馈）可延长交互，但检测率同步上升——揭示出欺骗深度与隐蔽性间的本质权衡。

Honeyval开源，为可信、可复现、面向攻防对抗的LLM安全应用评估树立新基准。

AI Summary (English)

Honeyval is the first comprehensive, automated, and verifiable evaluation framework for LLM-powered HTTP honeypots. It addresses critical limitations of prior work by (1) grounding evaluations in 16 realistic backend applications; (2) deploying AI hacking agents—not static scripts or humans—as adaptive attackers; (3) introducing two control tasks to independently monitor attacker capability and honeypot fidelity; and (4) defining clear, binary-exploit goals (e.g., reading /etc/passwd) for objective success measurement. Using Honeyval, we evaluate 7 cost-efficient LLMs (e.g., Qwen2-7B, Phi-3, Llama3-8B) as HTTP honeypots.
